# Command help documents config files and environment variables in Cobra's flags section.
exec mina help serve
stdout 'Serve the REST API'
stdout '--config-file'
stdout 'XDG_CONFIG_HOME/mina/config.toml'
stdout '--host'
stdout 'config: serve.host; env: MINA_HOST'
stdout 'env: MINA_YES'
stdout 'env: MINA_QUIET'
! stdout 'Precedence: defaults < config file < environment < CLI flags'

# The local config file supplies app settings; command-only settings come from command-owned env.
mkdir cfg/mina
cp config.toml cfg/mina/config.toml
env XDG_CONFIG_HOME=$WORK/cfg MINA_YES=true
exec mina migrate
exists cfg-serve.db

# Present config files are validated.
! exec mina --config-file invalid.toml migrate
stderr 'unsupported key unsupported'

# Environment values override the config file.
env MINA_DB=env-serve.db MINA_HOST=192.0.2.2 MINA_PORT=32002
! exec mina serve
stderr '192\.0\.2\.2:32002'
exists env-serve.db

# CLI flags override both environment values and the config file.
! exec mina serve --db cli-serve.db --host 192.0.2.3 --port 32003 --yes
stderr '192\.0\.2\.3:32003'
exists cli-serve.db

-- config.toml --
db = "cfg-serve.db"

[serve]
host = "192.0.2.1"
port = 32001

-- invalid.toml --
unsupported = true
